To increase this limit, please contact us.

system_updates.long_message:
As we continue to improve our products and features, we've also been working on updating our Terms and Conditions. You can now read our updated Terms and Conditions on our website.

If you have any questions, please contact support@artlogic.net
system_updates.short_message: Update to our Terms and Conditions

system_updates.long_message:
Starting February 6th, we will introduce two-factor authentication (2FA) as the default way to log into your database. You will have the option to set up 2FA by email or an authentication app on your phone.

Data privacy and security are our top priorities. This is why we are introducing a new way of logging in to your database. 2FA provides an extra layer of security to your account by sending a unique numerical code to a user’s email address or their chosen authenticator app during the login process. Only when entering your email, password, and the 2FA unique code, will you gain access to your account. This change will help protect your business and users by making it more difficult for someone else to sign in to your account.

Whilst 2FA will be required when logging into your account, you will have the option to select ‘Remember this device’ for up to 30 days, removing the need to use 2FA on every login.

After the 6th of February, you will be required to set up 2FA when you log in, and you will not be able to turn this feature off in the future, though you will be able to change 2FA methods/devices. If you have already set up two-factor authentication, nothing will change.

If you want to learn more about 2FA and how it can be implemented and managed for users, please visit our help guide.

If you have any questions or concerns about this change, please reach out to support@artlogic.net
system_updates.short_message: Two-factor authentication (2FA) is coming to your database soon

system_updates.long_message:
You now have the option to use email two-factor authentication (2FA) when logging into your database.

Email 2FA provides an extra layer of security to your account by sending a unique numerical code to a user’s email address during the login process. Only by entering your email, password, and the email 2FA unique code will give a user access to their account. This helps protect you by making it more difficult for someone else to sign in to your account. Currently, 2FA is not required on your account, however, early next year, we will require all users and databases to use a form of 2FA when logging in.

Before you can use the new email 2FA option, admins of your account will need to enable it. Once enabled, go to ‘Passwords & Security > Two-factor authentication’ and toggle on ‘Enable Two-factor authentication’ to begin using email 2FA. If you have any questions about resetting your 2FA, you will need to contact your system administrator.

You can read more about email 2FA on our help guide.

For more tips on ensuring your data and information stays safe, read our blog ‘4 easy steps to keeping your data safe’.

You can now personalise your database homepage with a banner image of your choice. To begin, click on the User icon in the top right and go to Customisation > Homepage Banner to upload your image.

Useful information

  • We recommend using an image of medium/high quality of at least 1000 x 1600 pixels.
  • The banner size is fixed and is only available horizontally. Depending on the orientation of your image, not all of it may be displayed. We recommend setting the focal point to ensure your favourite or important part is always visible.

Please note that the banner image will only affect your user account and will not appear for other team members using the organisation's database.

For more information about using the banner image tool or customising the appearance of your database, please read our help guide.

You can now select the artwork record stock numbers to be “Automatically created” when importing artworks using the artwork import tool. This uses the same functionality as the “Get next highest” stock number option when manually entering a new artwork record.

To use the artwork import tool, go to ‘Artwork > Tools > Import’ or learn more about using the import tool in our help guide.
system_updates.short_message: Automatically create stock numbers on import

system_updates.long_message:
This new optional synchronisation tool means you can keep artwork details updated on Artsy when you make changes to your artwork records in your database. When saving an artwork record in your database, you can now share all the new changes or only share selected fields to Artsy. You can also exclude specific artworks from using this feature. Changes made to artworks already published on Artsy will be reflected immediately on the marketplace. Unpublished artworks on your Artsy account can also be updated using this feature.

Before using this feature, we strongly recommend reading our help article.

To enable this feature go to 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core preferences > Integrations > Artlogic + Artsy integration' and select the preferences for automatic updates.

Watch our support webinar on how to enable and use this feature here.
system_updates.short_message: Artlogic + Artsy Integration 'Automatic synchronisation' feature now available

system_updates.long_message:
We have changed the way refer to 'Master' and 'Child' edition records.

  • Edition 'Master' records will now be known as the 'Edition Data Record'
  • 'Child' edition records will now be known as 'Edition records'

For more information on edition records, please visit our help guide: Prints & Editions Overview

When adding new contacts, a warning will now appear if a contact with the same email already exists in your database. This new feature will help you to avoid adding duplicate records.

Note: to view/resolve all duplicate contacts in your database, you can use the Resolve duplicates tool ('Contacts > Tools > Resolve duplicates').

Along with our friends at Artsy, we are very excited to introduce the Artlogic + Artsy integration, enabling you to seamlessly share selected artworks directly from your Artlogic Database to Artsy's global marketplace.

The integration is free to use and can be switched on today by admin users in your core preferences.

We are excited to announce that we have made an update to our website sharing screen in artwork records which will make it easier to share artworks from your database to an Artlogic website.

You currently do not have an Artlogic website connected to your database. To find out more about how Artlogic websites easily integrate with the database, please contact support. You can also join our 'Introduction to the new Website Sharing Screen' Support Webinar on Thursday 6th August 4 PM BST, to find out more about this feature - register here.

You can find more about Artlogic websites and online viewing rooms here.

To get started, please speak to our sales team today.

A new 'Website' tab in an artwork edit screen, will have further details of our website integration. System administrators can hide this tab by selecting 'Hide website tab from the artworks screen' in 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core Preferences > Artworks'

Users can now have the permission to send emails without also needing the permission to download/export contact information. Previously, users who were not allowed to download/export contacts could not send mailings even if 'Permit this user to send group emails' permission was on. As these permissions are now independent, we have disabled the 'Permit this user to send group emails' for anyone who also had 'Disallow download/export of all contacts reports' on their record. Admins can update a user with the new email sending permission on the 'Users' screen - Other > Admin > Users
For more information about managing users, please visit our help guide here.
system_updates.short_message: Email Sending Update to User Permissions

system_updates.long_message:
Invoice improvements

The following features are now available:

  • Select 'Show client email and telephone' in the invoice display settings to include additional contact details for clients in your invoices
  • Enable the 'Set invoice tax rate when adding artworks' preference to automatically set the invoice tax status to be consistent with the tax status of the added artwork(s)
  • Receive warnings on invoice creation if you have any of the following potential issues:
    • Your artwork has details which are not relevant to it's status e.g. a 'Stock' which has a consignment percentage set
    • Your artwork is listed as 'On consignment' but does not have the relevant consignment % or a net value entered
    • Your artwork has a different tax status to the tax status set on the invoice
    Disable these invoice warnings completely with the 'Hide invoice warnings' preference
System administrators can access the invoice preferences in 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core Preferences > Invoices'

Tax improvements

You can now change the following:

  • Set a tax status to be 'Tax inclusive'
  • Set a tax status to be 'Hidden on invoices' to ensure the tax payable is not itemised separately
  • Set a custom text to be displayed on invoices that have that tax status set

Learn more about the Invoice & Accounts section on our support website here.

Please note: Some of the features above require the applicable user settings for generating invoices and editing tax statuses.

The new Anti Money Laundering Directive 5 legislation is now in effect in the UK & EU. This requires Art Market Participants to carry out Customer Due Diligence (CDD) checks for both new and existing clients on transactions over € 10,000. We are introducing new tools in your database to help you stay in compliance with AMLD5.

You will be able to record the following details of CDD checks obtained for your clients; when the check was made, who performed the check and when it expires. This can be recorded directly in a contact record, in the 'Customer Due Diligence' section in the 'General' tab. An alert that these checks have been made will appear on the preview pane for that contact. These details are searchable and exportable.

For more advanced help notes on how to use and where to find our new tools then please visit our help guide for further details.

For more details on AMLD5 and what it means for you then visit our webpage artlogic.net/aml for further details.

If the AMLD5 compliance tools are not relevant to you then a system administrator can turn these off in 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core preferences > Contacts > Compliance Features'.
system_updates.short_message: Anti Money Laundering Directive 5 (AMLD5) compliance tools

system_updates.long_message:
Over the last few weeks, we have been inundated with support requests. Due to this considerable increase in requests, you may experience some delays in our response time when contacting our support team. This has been further impacted by our team working remotely, and some of them are working at a reduced capacity due to child care, self-isolation and caring for other family members affected by COVID-19.

We are committed to helping artists, galleries and collectors navigate this troubling and challenging time.

Please visit our webpage artlogic.net/covid19 for further information.

If you have any concerns or immediate questions, please do get in contact with us via support@artlogic.net

Due to the developing situation with the outbreak of COVID-19, we have implemented plans which ensure our services remain fully operational and supported by prioritising the health and wellbeing of our employees. As a precaution, we are advising all team members in our London and New York offices to work from home during this period.

While we anticipate that you won't experience any disruption to your Artlogic services during this time, we would ask that you understand if our support response times may take a little longer than normal.

We are monitoring the situation closely, and you can stay up to date with how we are responding to COVID-19 at artlogic.net/covid19.

You can now search invoices by 'Artist', 'Title', 'Stock number' and sundry item details. You can search for these fields using quick search or include them in more complex searches in 'Advanced search'.

The new Private Views edit screen is now live!

It offers many more ways to customise your Private View, including:

  • Custom header/background images, colours, fonts and layout
  • Dark theme
  • Secondary artwork images (including 'Installation views')
  • Document attachments
  • Social media sharing buttons

The completely redesigned screen also lets you see how your Private View looks as you build it.

For more information, please see our guide Creating a Private View

We hope you’ll find this new tool easy and enjoyable to use.

The old mailing composition tool has now been phased out.

Access the new mailing tool from the menu - go to ‘Sales & Marketing > Email marketing campaigns > Compose/edit emails’.

For further details please read our mailings guide. We hope you’ll find this new tool easy and enjoyable to use. If you need any help, please contact support.
system_updates.short_message: We have rebuilt the mailing tool

system_updates.long_message:
Enabling this option will allow you to directly request a shipping quote through ARTA from Artlogic.

The preference to enable this can be found in the 'Other' tab of 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core Preferences'.

See what artworks are in a contact’s collection. If the contact has been added to the ‘Current owner’ field in an artwork record, the artwork details will now be displayed in the ‘Purchases/collection’ tab of the contact’s edit screen.

Artworks displayed in the Private Views app or in PrivateView pages can have a grey dot ('other') in addition to the exisiting red ('unavailable') and yellow ('reserved') dots. The colour of the dot depend on the artwork's status and availability.

System administrators will be able to assign a status of 'Other' and check existing selections in the 'Private Views' tab of the 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core preferences' screen.

To coincide with the new 'Dark Mode' in Apple macOS Mojave launching this month, we're happy to introduce our new Dark Theme - perfect for night owls. You may select 'Dark Theme' from the User menu, just above 'Sign out'. Take a walk on the dark side!
You can now select your mailing campaign recipients by marketing & events list on the recipients tab of your mailing
system_updates.short_message: Select mailing recipients by Marketing & Events list

system_updates.long_message:
Enable a XLS export that you can use to import your artworks into Vastari.

The preference to enable this can be found in the 'Other' tab of 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core Preferences'.

The old mailing composition method is being phased out and will be completely removed on 30 September 2018. We suggest that you switch to the new Compose Email function before then. If you need any help, please contact support.

We’ve completely rebuilt the way you create emails, giving you more control over the appearance of your content. Get an instant preview of your email and change colours, fonts, spacing and more.

Access the new mailing tool from the menu - go to ‘Sales & Marketing > Email marketing campaigns > Compose/edit emails’.

For further details please read our mailings guide. We hope you’ll find this new tool easy and enjoyable to use.
system_updates.short_message: We have rebuilt the mailing tool

system_updates.long_message:
Enable a CSV export that you can use to import your artworks into Articheck to create digital condition reports.

The preference to enable this can be found in the 'Other' tab of 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core Preferences'.

Autopopulate your contact addresses using the 'Search for an address' field on the contact edit screen.

You can disable this with the 'Disable Google Places autocomplete' preference found in 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core Preferences'.
system_updates.short_message: 'Google Places' autocomplete added to Contact address

system_updates.long_message:
Create relationships between artworks with the new 'Grouped editions' and 'Related artworks' features. Set and manage these in the artwork preview pane.

You can disable these with the 'Hide Grouped editions on Preview Pane' and 'Hide Related artworks on Preview Pane' preferences found in 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core Preferences'.

You can now upload email attachments when sending emails from:

  • 'Contacts > Send Emails > Compose a new email'
  • 'Artworks > Sell/Offer/Share > Email selected items'

An option to give Artwork or Contact records a rating of up to five stars based on how important they are. You can view and edit this on the list screen and the preview pane, as well as using it for sorting.
system_updates.short_message: Ability to add 'Importance' to Artwork and Contact records

system_updates.long_message: Margin Scheme calculations have been improved to take into account 'Production costs' for 'On consignment' artworks. For further information please see the 'info' button alongside the 'Cost price' field in the 'Financial' tab of an artwork record.

Users with Admin or 'Accounts' privileges will have access to a selection of new accounting reports that can be generated from 'Accounts > Accounts Reports and Exports > Download Report...'

  • Cost of Sales report
  • Accounts Receivable report
  • Sales Tax report
  • Payments history

System administrators will find more options in 'Other > Admin > Preferences > Core preferences'.
                    These will give you more control over the way you use Artlogic's features.
system_updates.short_message: More flexibility with managing your preferences

system_updates.long_message:
We have implemented some feature to help you manage your data protection compliancy. These include:

  • A Data Protection dashboard in 'Contacts > Data Protection > Dashboard' to help you keep on top of data protection or GDPR compliance in your country.
  • A 'Data protection information' section in the 'Categories & Interests' tab of a contact record where you can record data protection information including the 'Legal basis of consent'.
  • More specific mailing preferences so that your clients can choose whether they wanted to be contacted through mass and/or personal emails.
  • A 'GDPR/ Data protection report' that can be run on an individual client-by-client basis. Find this in the 'Action/ Downloads' section on a contact's preview pane.

See our website for further details about these features and how they relate to Data protection / GDPR regulations.

The new mailing compose tool is now available as a beta feature.

We've completely rebuilt the way you create your emails, giving you more control over the appearance of your content. Get an instant preview of your email and change colours, fonts, spacing and more.

Access the new mailing tool from the menu - go to Sales & Marketing > Email marketing campaigns > Compose/edit emails [new version]

You can now create a batch of artworks by uploading images, using the new upload & create multiple feature.
                    Just go to the artworks page and click on 'New Record' > 'Upload & create multiple'. From there you can select
                    images to upload and then enter the details of each work.
system_updates.short_message: Batch upload images and create multiple artworks

system_updates.long_message:
A new version of the 'ArtlogicHelper' extension for Chrome is available. The Chrome Extension allows you to batch download artwork images from artwork list screens.

New in this version:

  • Download the original images as well as three pre-defined sizes
  • Download secondary images if desired.

      • Read more and install in the Chrome browser here:

      https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/artlogic-helper/nklbnijjgbbclhojjpbckmbcooegepbn.
system_updates.short_message: New version of Artlogic Chrome Extension available

system_updates.long_message: We've added a Data Protection dashboard in 'Contacts > Data Protection > Dashboard' to help you keep on top of data protection or GDPR compliance in your country.

System administrators can now manage word templates for reports and invoices. These can be set in preferences, found at ‘Other’ > ‘Admin’ > ‘Preferences’.

For more information view the document templates guide, found in the help menu.
system_updates.short_message: Update your own headers and footers

system_updates.long_message:
When an invoice is partially paid the payments received will be displayed and deducted from the invoice total, creating a new invoice due amount in the invoice screen and on downloaded invoice documents.
This can be suppressed with the ‘Hide payment received‘ checkbox found in ‘Settings’ > ‘Display settings’ in an invoice record.
system_updates.short_message: Show payments received on partially paid invoices

system_updates.long_message: You will find the new import tool under ‘Tools’ > ‘Import (beta)’ in either the ‘Contacts’, ‘Artworks’ or ‘Library and publications’ menu. Use this to import records from an excel spreadsheet into Artlogic.
You will find these new fields in a contact record under ‘contact’ tab within the ‘General’ section.
system_updates.short_message: Record the 'Spoken language', 'Date of birth' of a contact.

system_updates.long_message: You now can block a contact by selecting the ‘do not contact’ preference in ‘Settings’ section of a contact record. In addition, there is also a ‘Blocked from invoicing’ check box. Once selected, you will not be able to add this contact to an invoice.
system_updates.short_message:
New settings to prevent invoicing bad risk clients and to
                    'block' a contact.

system_updates.long_message: Before you download a Presentation document or Works available list, you can choose to remove ‘.00’ from the prices.
system_updates.short_message:
Optionally remove the '.00' from prices on Presentation and
                    Works available documents.

system_updates.long_message: The ‘Hide signed and dated details’ check box is under the ‘artwork’ tab of an artwork record.
system_updates.short_message:
Hide the 'Signed and dated' field of an artwork from the core
                    artwork details (for Private Views, documents etc.)

system_updates.long_message: Now you can more easily track the artworks you have reserved for clients under ‘Artworks’ > ‘Deadlines’ > ‘Reserves’ and view the works based on offer due dates.
system_updates.short_message:
Help checking Reserves: New views of 'Reserved' works within
                    'Artworks' > ‘Deadlines’.

system_updates.long_message: On the invoice edit screen, after you have selected the client you are invoicing to, you can click on ‘Change address’ to switch between the multiple addresses you have saved for this contact. If you want to add a shipping address for the contact that is not saved, just click on ‘Add shipping address’ and add in the new address.
system_updates.short_message:
More flexibility with invoice addresses; show two addresses
                    (shipping and billing) and switch between them easily.

system_updates.long_message: Your sales team can now indicate the artworks they have sold so the accounts team can find these sold works and generate invoices for them. Simply do this by changing the artwork’s ‘Status and availability’ to ‘Ex-inventory - Sold’ and an ‘Invoice pending’ check box will appear below. The check box should be automatically ticked and the name of the user who changed the status will show in the ‘Sold by’ field.
system_updates.short_message: Record that an artwork is sold pending an invoice.

system_updates.long_message: Now when you click on an artist in the ‘Artist’ section of the database, you will find different views such as ‘All works’, ‘Available works’, ‘Reserved works’, ‘Unsold works’ and ‘Sold works’ to help you quickly find the type of artworks you need.
system_updates.short_message:
Improvements to finding artworks by artists on the 'Artists’
                    page.

system_updates.long_message: On artwork list screens, hover your mouse over the coloured dots that represent the artworks's status to see what the dot represents (available, sold, etc.). Click the 'info' button for a full explanation of all possible availabilities.

We've moved 'Safe mode' from the User menu at the top of the screen to a new 'settings' panel at the bottom of list screens. Look out for the 'toggle' icon at the bottom right of list screens:  

Safe mode allows you to hide sensitive information like Prices on list screens. You may type 'h' ('hide') to toggle Safe Mode.

You may also turn on 'Expanded' views here, or type 'x' to toggle it on or off. Expanded views are useful where you need to see more information than is visible on a single line.

Perhaps our most significant new feature since creating Private View links. Users are now able to select artworks in the database and then create a tailored sales email that can be sent directly to one or multiple clients. All emails are tracked and can also be turned into an offer. The combination of sales emails and subsequent offers provides the most powerful sales pipeline management tool we have ever created. Sales emails can be created by flagging artworks and then going to ‘Sell/Offer/Share > Email Artworks’. Recipients can be selected via the usual searching and flagging methods. Messages can use insertion tags (such as the contacts first name) so multiple recipients will receive what will look like a personal email.
system_updates.short_message: Sales/offer Emails - create and send offer emails directly from the system.

system_updates.long_message: Contacts can now be linked to one another in two different ways. Firstly, you can turn a contact record into an ‘Organisation’ and then then attach ‘Members’ (employees) to the organisation. This will appear in the ‘Related contacts’ tab, on the list screen and edit screen. Inside an organisation record you can also view the purchase histories of all its members. Additionally, contacts can be connected to one another as a ‘related’ contact and a relationship type can be added. You might want to relate an art advisor and a collector or a husband and wife.
system_updates.short_message: Related Contacts - Group contacts based on relationships and organisations.

system_updates.long_message: It is now possible to import artworks and contacts through our dramatically improved import tools. Simply organise your data into excel spreadsheets and upload them directly into Artlogic. Images can also be imported. You'll find the import tools here: ‘Contacts > Tools > Import’ or ‘Artworks > Tools > Import’.
system_updates.short_message: Import Tools - bulk import contacts and artworks data from excel spreadsheets.

system_updates.long_message: Following other improvements to production and additional costs calculations in the artwork financial tab, we are now able to accurately show predicted profit prior to a sale and the achieved profit once a sale is officially made.
system_updates.short_message: Profitability Graph - see your profit for each artwork before and after the sale.

system_updates.long_message: In the financial tab of an artwork record you can now itemise production costs in much greater detail, keeping track of the original cost/currency, who paid it and convert the amount in to the sale currency of the artwork.
system_updates.short_message: Advanced Production Costs - manage production costs in original currency and sale currency.

system_updates.long_message: With dramatically increased offer activity, thanks to the brand new sales email feature, we have now created ‘Offer status’ to help with managing your sales pipeline. You can create your own offer statuses and pick a corresponding coloured dot. This allows you to grade your offers based on their level of importance/urgency. You'll find offer statuses, here: ‘Sales & Marketing > Sales & Offers > Offer Statuses’.
system_updates.short_message: Offer Status Tracking - create offer statuses to label offers by levels of importance.

system_updates.long_message: Whenever an artworks status is changed to sold or reserved the database will now automatically input the Artlogic user’s name next to the status in the artwork financial tab.
system_updates.short_message: Sold by & reserved by - automatically record the user who sold or reserved the artwork.

system_updates.long_message: You will find a new field within the ‘List and categories’ tab on the preview pane that displays which artists a contact owns works by. This is an automatic field that is generated by searching through data in the contact’s purchases history.
system_updates.short_message: Own works by - see the artists a contact has purchased works from in preview pane.

system_updates.long_message: Get the most out of Artlogic by visiting our Vimeo channel for a full set of training videos. Topics include 'Getting started', 'Artworks', 'Prints & Editions', 'Contacts', 'Searching & Flagging' and more...
system_updates.short_message: We've launched a set of training videos on Vimeo.

system_updates.long_message: We've added a new feature to the PrivateViews app: Press and hold images when in detail view to save them to your device, or share them via Message, Mail, Facebook, Twitter, WhatsApp and many other supported applications.
system_updates.short_message: Press and hold to save and share images in the PrivateViews app.

system_updates.long_message:
We have added a new view of all your uploaded images, whether they were uploaded from artworks, contacts or another part of the system. You'll find this in the main menu, here: 'Other > Documents & files > Uploaded images'.

If available, you'll be able to see metadata here such as EXIF data, as well as colour data. You'll also be able to download the original as well as add captions and tags to help you search for images.

Please note that if the images were uploaded on the old system only limited data will be available.

We'll be building more functionality into this section next year so watch this space!

You may now include artist dates in the artwork details that are exported to the Private Views iPad app.

Go to 'Artworks' > 'Private Views' > 'iPad/iPhone synchronisation and select 'Export Artwork dates' in the 'Artwork export settings' section.

If you're sending email campaigns in Artlogic 3 (Artlogic Pro) or using our new 'Email selected items...' feature in Artworks, we've added some great new tools so you can view email metrics, including delivered, opened, clicked and bounced. We're also added an 'Email bounce management' tool to help you clean up your mailing list, helping you to automatically remove or edit bounced emails.

You'll find these tools in the main menu: 'Sales & marketing > Email marketing campaigns > Email logs & analytics'.

You may now sort artworks within an artwork list which allows you to generate reports and exports with a custom sort order.

Open the edit screen of an artwork list and drag and drop the artworks into the order you prefer, then click on the 'View' button in the list and select 'View list'. This will take you to a list of the artworks from where you may generate Reports and exports in the custom order.

Users may now receive email notifications when payments are applied to invoices in the Accounts section of Artlogic.

To add a user the list of recipients, go to 'Other > Admin > Users', open the user and tick the relevant checkbox in the User Settings section.

When a payment is applied to an invoice, the user entering the payment will be prompted to confirm that notifications should be sent and a custom message may be entered if desired.

We've added a new feature that allows you to automatically email a request for a shipping quote for a single artwork or a group of artworks, including packing details. The email can be sent to a default shipper or to any chosen email address.

For a single artwork, select 'Request shipping quote' in the right hand preview pane. For a group of artworks, select the artworks using advanced search or flags, click the 'Create...' button at the bottom of the screen, and select 'Documents/reports', then 'Request shipping quote'. This will bring up a screen from which the shipper and sender email addresses, email text, and artwork packing details can all be edited. A default shipper email address may be set by admin users by selecting the following main menu option: 'Other > Admin > Preferences'.

Shipping quotes are saved in the system (in 'Other > Admin > Shipping quotes') so admin users can see which ones have been sent.

We're excited to launch a new 'Artists' feature on the main menu so you may browse works in your inventory by artist.

This page displays the image of the most recent artwork for each artist. Click an image to browse works or click the pencil to edit the record, for instance to set the artist as a 'Gallery artist'.

To select a specific image for that artist, check the 'Artist's representative work' checkbox in the first tab of an artwork record.

Today we've added support for drag & drop image ordering.

Just drag a single image or select multiple by clicking on the checkbox below each image and then and drop them into place.

In order to streamline the bug fixing process and to provide the most accurate information on any issues you experience, we have set up an easy to use ‘Report a problem’ form. You can access it by clicking the button in bottom right corner of the homepage.

Please be sure to give us as much information about the problem you have encountered. If possible please provide us with the following information:

  1. The section of the database where the error occurred.
  2. The work flow that caused the problems.
  3. References to the specific records you were working on i.e. the stock number or the exact contacts name.
  4. Screen shots of the the problems you encountered.
  5. The web browser you were using at the time.

The more detailed you can be the better but of course don’t worry if you can’t provide all of the above information.

Thank you very much for your participation!

Today we have launched Artlogic 3 Beta. We are still intensively working on the system so please look out for anything that seems out of place or incorrect. If you come across issues please do get in contact with us over email (support@artlogic.net) or (+44 207 112 8825).

As we add more features, fix issues and generally polish the system, we will add updates into this section. We thank you for using the beta version of Artlogic 3 and for your patience while we work towards finishing the system.
